blocks.content: Combat Readiness (3)

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: That's incredible, Fermi! How much did you compress the air to reach 1,000 press?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yeah, even if it hits armor, the impact would be significant.
Shirone was unimpressed.
In the past, he would have at least clapped, but it didn't compare to Gaold's air gun.
Fermi steadily conquered the master difficulty.
When he finished the 10th level of the movement category, performing acrobatic flying, applause erupted.

The level of skill required to spiral up like a screw, draw a large circle, and then freely glide through the air like a pinwheel was impossible without mastering the basics of both press and blow categories.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Fermi, all master. 100 points.
It was the moment Fermi reclaimed the top spot in the graduating class.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's certainly impressive.
It was rare for Iruki to acknowledge someone.
This time, even Shirone had no grounds to argue.
It was an all-master achievement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Indeed. His fundamentals seem very solid.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, that's not what I mean. Fermi was an all-master last year too. Just in a different major.
Shirone quickly turned his head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A different major?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yes. He registered for earth magic. The year before, he was an all-master in fire magic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could it really be possible? Changing majors every year and mastering all of them?
For a high-level magician, student-level difficulties might be manageable, but it was an unfair comparison.
Fermi was a student.
Shirone stared blankly at Fermi.
When Fermi made a throat-slitting gesture with his thumb, the emotions from the survival evaluation resurfaced.
Fermi had achieved all-master and became the undisputed number one.
ΓÇö It's too late to regret, rookie. I won't let you graduate until you lick my shoes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Reading the anger in Fermi's eyes, Shirone stood up and spoke to his friends as he headed to the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Iruki, Nade. I'll go up first.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: What is he thinking? A head-on confrontation won't benefit him. It'll just expose his abilities. If he fails, he'll be completely crushed.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: He must have a plan. Let's trust Shirone.
The students' attention focused on Shirone.
Amy and Arin turned their heads from their conversation, and Canis's eyes sharpened.
Dante, sitting on the ground, propped his chin and watched.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Fermi achieved all-master. How will you respond?
The evaluator held the evaluation sheet and asked.

blocks.speaker: Evaluator
blocks.content: Shirone, your major is Divine Particle. What are your challenge tasks?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Power level 7, projectile speed level 8, firing speed level 7, defense level 7...
Up to week 7, Shirone's average special skill evaluation level was 3.6.
The levels he was now reciting were more than double that.

blocks.speaker: Evaluator
blocks.content: Lastly, movement control, level 10.
The evaluator's eyes sparkled.
Level 10 was master difficulty.
Moreover, the movement control category for Divine Particle had much higher standards than the photon category.
It was clear that Fermi's all-master had influenced Shirone's decision.
However, it was also true that this was insufficient to compete with Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Evaluator
blocks.content: What is this? If you're going to do it, do it properly. This is half-hearted.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: He's being stubborn. He needs to follow to some extent so that other students will be conflicted between Fermi and Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: If that's the case, it's a huge mistake. This won't even scratch Fermi. It's a typical first-year mistake. Freshmen can't help it.
This was the opinion of most of Class Two.
Dante withheld judgment and fell into thought.
His lips curled as he quickly blinked and calculated.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, that method...
Meanwhile, Fermi's group watching from the hill already had faces of assured victory.

blocks.speaker: Lycan
blocks.content: Heh, is this his last struggle? It's kind of cute, isn't it, Fermi?
Electric monster Lycan looked at Fermi.
Fermi was smiling.
But Lycan, who had spent years with him, knew it was the smile he showed when he was most furious.

blocks.speaker: Lycan
blocks.content: 'That damn rookie...'
Shirone's first evaluation was the single attribute power category.
This category didn't exist in the photon series but was newly established based on the mass of the Divine Particle.
As he closed his eyes and concentrated, a sphere of light was born, compressing at a terrifying speed as if pushing space away.
Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang!
The flash embedded in the glass orb spread into fragments of light, and the gauge spun to calculate the power.
2,242 crash.
It far exceeded the 1,800 crash standard for level 7 single attribute power.
When Shirone checked the score and looked at Fermi, the students finally realized something and began to murmur.
When converted to energy units, 2,242 crash was roughly equivalent to 8,200 press.
In other words, the destructive power of compressing the atmosphere with 8,200 press was 2,240 crash.
This was 7.3 times higher than Fermi's 1,123 press, showcasing the power of the Divine Particle.
Shirone's evaluation continued.
In the projectile speed category, the photon cannon's speed was an impressive 100 meters per second, far surpassing Fermi's projectile speed.
Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang!
Shirone fired the photon cannon at the target ahead at a tremendous speed.
Even in broad daylight, the surroundings flashed, and the students' vision was filled with golden streaks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 165 rounds per minute. Level 7 passed.
Fermi's master level for firing speed was 160 rounds per minute.
Having already surpassed Fermi in three categories, the students now grasped Shirone's intention.
I see. That guy Shirone...
The seven tasks Shirone presented as challenge tasks were also common to the air category.
He's surpassing all of Fermi's evaluation tasks.
When the students turned to Fermi, he was watching Shirone with his face covered by his palm.
Only his close companions knew that his eyes were burning with murderous intent.
Shirone didn't achieve all-master.
But even so, he scored higher in the same categories than Fermi, who did achieve all-master.
The students felt the same.
Air magic could be overwhelmed by the Divine Particle at level 7.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: That damn bastard...
Fermi's companions were startled.
It was the first time his emotions were so blatantly exposed.
Feeling the gazes of many students, Fermi finally lowered his hand and smiled.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: 'Oh, right, the business smile. The business smile.'
A businessman must remain composed.
Even if debt is choking them and a knife is about to stab their back, confidence can open a way out.
Of course, the crisis was real.
In reality, students were halving their expectations of Fermi's success and shifting them to Shirone.
The air category was at the level of the Divine Particle at level 7.
The strategy of raising his stock through comparison with Fermi's scores worked perfectly.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Shirone is truly amazing.
Aider approached from behind Iruki.
His major was also in the air category, so it was natural to feel bitter.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: His talent is different. How can we beat someone like that?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Talent?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Yes. The advantage of the photon category is that it has no mass.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: But Shirone can even add mass to it. No one can beat that.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Talent is just a tool.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: The speed of digging the ground with bare hands versus with a shovel is different.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: If the ground is magic, the shovel is talent.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: But no matter how much you have a shovel, the ground won't be dug unless you use your arms, waist, and legs.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: I don't think anyone is born empty-handed.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Some are born with shovels, some with hammers, some with pens.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Those with shovels can dig the ground faster, but they can't write well with them.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Talent isn't given; it's found and developed by oneself.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: The air category has something only it can do.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Just because you haven't found it doesn't mean you should take lightly what others have found.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Talent is also effort.
Aider pondered those words.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Yes. Thank you, Iruki.
Iruki raised and lowered his eyebrows.
His lack of offense and rather grateful attitude was indeed fitting for the youngest.
It was why Aider had no enemies.

blocks.speaker: Evaluator
blocks.content: Lastly, movement control. Master difficulty. Reaction speed must be below 0.8 seconds.
Shirone took a deep breath and composed himself.
Although he felt dizzy after exerting himself in six categories, it was nothing compared to his past practical experiences.
The evaluator activated the Red Line certified evaluation magic, Navigation.
A small sphere was born, and inside it, an arrow indicating direction spun like a compass that had lost its polarity.
Movement control is a category where the magician must accurately execute the movements suggested by the navigation.
The shape, length, and color of the arrow each represent direction, distance, and speed, and the magician must perform them within the reaction time.
The master difficulty for Divine Particle is a reaction time of 0.8 seconds.
This is 0.2 seconds shorter than the master difficulty for the photon series, which is 1 second.
At the neural level, 0.2 seconds is a significant gap.
Nevertheless, the association set this standard because they believed rapid acceleration and deceleration using mass would be possible.

blocks.speaker: Evaluator
blocks.content: Reaction time of 0.8 seconds?
The students looked bewildered.
Photon series magicians, of course, differentiate time to pre-construct a series of processes.
Applications like Patrol and Rainbow Drop come from this.
However, movement control is not a category where you move as you think.
Navigation will suggest directions, speeds, and distances that are completely unexpected, and the magician must react immediately.
In such a situation, demanding a reaction time of 0.8 seconds seemed too harsh.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'No, the association's judgment is correct.'
Shirone recalled Collie's teachings.
The standard exists because someone is achieving it.
He had heard that Etella's reaction time for movement control, called superhuman, was 0.1 seconds.
At that level, one could traverse densely wooded areas with just teleportation.
It was also said that one could dodge attacks with just upper body weaving by repeatedly teleporting in place.
Of course, as a student, Shirone couldn't take comfort in what a renowned monk on the continent could do.
But he understood the association's thinking correctly.
Do not judge Divine Particle by the standards of the photon series.
It was a unique ability that only Shirone possessed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'I will conquer it with my skills.'
Light rose from Shirone's body like flames.
The flames, growing in size, became a pair of giant wings that burned brightly.
What, what is that?
The students' eyes widened.
It was magic they had never seen or even heard of before.
